name: Moanalua Valley Trail
tagline: A wild 10-mile valley-to-summit trek through stream crossings and native forest to the Ko'olau ridge.
parking: Free parking at the end of Moanalua Valley Road — the neighborhood gate is open during daylight hours
bestTime: Start early — this is a long day hike and you want to summit before afternoon clouds
talkStory: About 10 miles round trip with 2,600 ft elevation gain to the Ko'olau summit. Expect stream crossings (at least 12), muddy conditions year-round, and rope sections near the top. The valley itself is stunning with native plants and old taro patches. The Ko'olau summit views of windward Oahu are breathtaking. This is a full-day commitment — bring lunch, 3+ liters of water, and start by 7am. Not for casual hikers, but the reward at the top is unreal.
